devanagari: अवधूतस्य अजगरादि नवगुरूणां वर्णनम् । श्रीब्राह्मण उवाच । सुखम् ऐन्द्रियकं राजन् स्वर्गे नरक एव च । देहिनां यद् यथा दुःखं तस्मान् नेच्छेत...
english_devnagari: avadhūtasya ajagarādi navagurūṇāṃ varṇanam śrībrāhmaṇa uvāca sukham aindriyakaṃ rājan svarge naraka eva ca dehināṃ yad yathā duḥkhaṃ tasmān neccheta tad‍budhaḥ

translation:
The Brāhmaṇa continued:
Pleasure as well as pain engendered by the senses may be experienced in the celestial and internal regions by all embodied beings. A wise man, therefore, should not hanker after them, O King,

devanagari: ग्रासं सुमृष्टं विरसं महान्तं स्तोकमेव वा । यदृच्छयैवापतितं ग्रसेत् आजगरोऽक्रियः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: grāsaṃ sumṛṣṭaṃ virasaṃ mahāntaṃ stokameva vā yadṛcchayaivāpatitaṃ graset ājagaro
translation: Like a boa-constrictor lying without any effort or activity, a sage should subsist himself on whatever food (providentially) comes to him (without any effort), irrespective of its taste (whether extremely delicious or distasteful) or of the quantity obtained (whether much or little).

devanagari: शयीताहानि भूरीणि निराहारोऽनुपक्रमः । यदि नोपनमेद् ग्रासो महाहिरिव दिष्टभुक् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śayītāhāni bhūrīṇi nirāhāro yadi nopanamed grāso mahāhiriva diṣṭabhuk
translation: If no food comes to the lot of a sage who vows to live only on the food that Providence sends to him, he should not exert for it, but like his (preceptor)—huge serpent, should lie down for days together without food but should not strive for it.

devanagari: ओजःसहोबलयुतं बिभ्रद् देहमकर्मकम् । शयानो वीतनिद्रश्च नेहेतेन्द्रियवानपि ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ojaḥsahobalayutaṃ bibhrad dehamakarmakam śayāno vītanidraśca nehetendriyavānapi

translation: Though endowed with a body full of physical strength, mental energy and soundness of sense-organs, the sage should lie down remaining actionless yet sleepless. He should not strive to do anything for food, though he may possess powers of his sense-organs.

devanagari: मुनिः प्रसन्नगम्भीरो दुर्विगाह्यो दुरत्ययः । अनन्तपारो ह्यक्षोभ्यः स्तिमितोद इवार्णवः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: muniḥ prasannagambhīro durvigāhyo duratyayaḥ anantapāro hyakṣobhyaḥ stimitoda ivārṇavaḥ
translation: Like the limitless, unfathomable, uṇperturbable deep sea of clear translucent water, the sage should be quiet, absorbed in meditation, unviolable, inscrutable, unaffected by time, space, etc. and unperturbed by passions.

devanagari: समृद्धकामो हीनो वा नारायणपरो मुनिः । नोत्सर्पेत न शुष्येत सरिद्भिरिव सागरः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: samṛddhakāmo hīno vā nārāyaṇaparo muniḥ notsarpeta na śuṣyeta saridbhiriva sāgaraḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ hā class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ hā class 3 verb]
[optative active second plural], [optative passive third single]

translation: Just as a sea does not overflow its limit when rivers in floods fall into it, nor does it dry up with the drying up of rivers, the sage should not feel elated when his desires arc fulfilled nor be depressed at disappointments, but should be completely devoted to Nārāyaṇa (and not pay attention to worldly objects).

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा स्त्रियं देवमायां तद्भावैरजितेन्द्रियः । प्रलोभितः पतत्यन्धे तमस्यग्नौ पतङ्गवत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā striyaṃ devamāyāṃ tadbhāvairajitendriyaḥ pralobhitaḥ patatyandhe tamasyagnau pataṅgavat

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [locative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: On seeing a women, a veritable deluding potency of God, a person who has control over his senses is fascinated with her alluring gestures and movements and like a moth flying into the fire, falls into dark hell.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a famous verse which describes how a moth, a black-bee, an elephant, a ḍcer and a fish fall a victim severally to the objects of senses like a form, scent, touch, sound and taste and the five symbolic preceptors from this verse onwards are meant to teach detachment from objects of senses.
The verse quoted is as follows:
pataṅga-mātaṅga-kuraṅga-bhṛṅga-mīnā hatāḥ pañcabhir eva pañca /
ekaḥ pramādī sa kathaṃ na hanyate, yaḥ sevate pañcabhir eva pañca”

devanagari: योषिद्धिरण्याभरणाम्बरादि । द्रव्येषु मायारचितेषु मूढः । प्रलोभितात्मा ह्युपभोगबुद्ध्या । पतङ्गवत् नश्यति नष्टदृष्टिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: yoṣiddhiraṇyābharaṇāmbarādi dravyeṣu māyāraciteṣu mūḍhaḥ pralobhitātmā hyupabhogabuddhyā pataṅgavat naśyati naṣṭadṛṣṭiḥ
translation: With his mind enticed by women, (wearing) gold ornaments, rich dress, and other things created by the deluding Potency of the Lord (Māyā), the infatuated person, losing his discrimination, regards them for his enjoyment and ruins himself like a moth in the fire.

devanagari: स्तोकं स्तोकं ग्रसेद् ग्रासं देहो वर्तेत यावता । गृहान् अहिंसन् आतिष्ठेद् वृत्तिं माधुकरीं मुनिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: stokaṃ stokaṃ grased grāsaṃ deho varteta yāvatā gṛhān ahiṃsan ātiṣṭhed vṛttiṃ mādhukarīṃ muniḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: Without causing any trouble to any nouseholder, a sage should accept food only in small quantities and that too, barely sufficient for the maintenance of his body. In this respect he should follow the course of a bee.

devanagari: अणुभ्यश्च महद्‍भ्यश्च शास्त्रेभ्यः कुशलो नरः । सर्वतः सारमादद्यात् पुष्पेभ्य इव षट्पदः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: aṇubhyaśca mahad‍bhyaśca śāstrebhyaḥ kuśalo naraḥ sarvataḥ sāramādadyāt puṣpebhya iva ṣaṭpadaḥ
translation: Just as a bcc collects honey from flowers big or small, a skilful and clever person should assimilate the essence of the Śāstras great and small.

devanagari: सायन्तनं श्वस्तनं वा न सङ्गृह्णीत भिक्षितम् । पाणिपात्रोदरामत्रो मक्षिकेव न सङ्ग्रही ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sāyantanaṃ śvastanaṃ vā na saṅgṛhṇīta bhikṣitam pāṇipātrodarāmatro makṣikeva na saṅgrahī

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ bhaj ], [accusative single from √ bhaj ]

translation: A recluse should not accept alms with a view to storing (a part of) it, for the evening or for the next day. He should use no other vessel than his palms (for accepting food) and his stomach (as storage). He ought not to be a hoarder like the bee.

devanagari: सायन्तनं श्वस्तनं वा न सङ्गृह्णीत भिक्षुकः । मक्षिका इव सङ्गृह्णन् सह तेन विनश्यति ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sāyantanaṃ śvastanaṃ vā na saṅgṛhṇīta bhikṣukaḥ makṣikā iva saṅgṛhṇan saha tena vinaśyati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation: A recluse should not lay by any food for the evening or for the morrow. If he were to do so like a bee, he will perish along with his store.

devanagari: पदापि युवतीं भिक्षुः न स्पृशेद् दारवीमपि । स्पृशन् करीव बध्येत करिण्या अङ्गसङ्गतः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: padāpi yuvatīṃ bhikṣuḥ na spṛśed dāravīmapi spṛśan karīva badhyeta kariṇyā aṅgasaṅgataḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: A mendicant should not touch, even with his foot, a young woman (doll made of wood), for he will be bound (with passion) like an elephant fettered while touching a female elephant!

devanagari: नाधिगच्छेत् स्त्रियं प्राज्ञः कर्हिचित् मृत्युमात्मनः । बलाधिकैः स हन्येत गजैरन्यैर्गजो यथा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: nādhigacchet striyaṃ prājñaḥ karhicit mṛtyumātmanaḥ balādhikaiḥ sa hanyeta gajairanyairgajo yathā
translation: A wise person should never try to contact a woman (of another person) for he should know that she is his veritable death, just as an elephant is killed by more powerful tuskers.

devanagari: न देयं नोपभोग्यं च लुब्धैर्यद् दुःखसञ्चितम् । भुङ्क्ते तदपि तच्चान्यो मधुहेवार्थविन्मधु ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: na deyaṃ nopabhogyaṃ ca lubdhairyad duḥkhasañcitam bhuṅkte tadapi taccānyo madhuhevārthavinmadhu

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ lubh class 6 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ lubh class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ lubh class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Wealth hoarded by greedy misers painstakingly is neither enjoyed by them nor it is given in charities but it is enjoyed by another powerful person (knowing the location of the treasure) and that also by still another even as a gatherer of honey knows the place of the honeycomb (the store of honey) by external indications and misappropriates it.

devanagari: सुदुःखोपार्जितैः वित्तैः आशासानां गृहाशिषः । मधुहेवाग्रतो भुङ्क्ते यतिर्वै गृहमेधिनाम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: suduḥkhopārjitaiḥ vittaiḥ āśāsānāṃ gṛhāśiṣaḥ madhuhevāgrato bhuṅkte yatirvai gṛhamedhinām

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Just as a honey-gatherer (is the first enjoyer of honey collected by bees) a recluse is the first to enjoy the fruit (i.e. food prepared by householders) of the wealth amassed by the householders with great pains and which they expect to enjoy.

devanagari: ग्राम्यगीतं न श्रृणुयाद् यतिर्वनचरः क्वचित् । शिक्षेत हरिणाद् बद्धान् मृगयोर्गीतमोहितात् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: grāmyagītaṃ na śrṛṇuyād yatirvanacaraḥ kvacit śikṣeta hariṇād baddhān mṛgayorgītamohitāt

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]
[optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: From the deer which got ensnared by being beguiled with the music (musical notes) of the hunter, an ascetic living in a forest should learn the lesson that he should not listen to vulgar songs (creating erotic passions). (He should rather listen to songs about the Lord).

devanagari: नृत्यवादित्रगीतानि जुषन् ग्राम्याणि योषिताम् । आसां क्रीडनको वश्य ऋष्यश्रृङ्गो मृगीसुतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: nṛtyavāditragītāni juṣan grāmyāṇi yoṣitām āsāṃ krīḍanako vaśya ṛṣyaśrṛṅgo mṛgīsutaḥ
translation: It was while enjoying the vulgar dance, instrumental music and songs of young women that Ṛṣyaśṛṅga, the son of a female deer (from sage Vibhāṇḍaka) became a toy in the hands of those women.

devanagari: जिह्वयातिप्रमाथिन्या जनो रसविमोहितः । मृत्युम् ऋच्छत्यसद्‍बुधिः मीनस्तु बडिशैर्यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: jihvayātipramāthinyā jano rasavimohitaḥ mṛtyum ṛcchatyasad‍budhiḥ mīnastu baḍiśairyathā
translation: A person being tempted by alluring tastes through his uncontrolled tongue, loses his reasoning capacity and meets his end like a thoughtless fish allured by baits.

devanagari: इन्द्रियाणि जयन्त्याशु निराहारा मनीषिणः । वर्जयित्वा तु रसनं तन्निरन्नस्य वर्धते ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: indriyāṇi jayantyāśu nirāhārā manīṣiṇaḥ varjayitvā tu rasanaṃ tannirannasya vardhate

translation: By observing fast wise persons quickly bring under control all their senses except the tongue (the sense of taste) which grows all the more powerful during famishment.

devanagari: तावत् जितेन्द्रियो न स्याद् विजितान्येन्द्रियः पुमान् । न जयेद् रसनं यावत् जितं सर्वं जिते रसे ॥ २१ ॥

translation: Even if a person has controlled all other senses (except the tongue), he cannot really be said to have subdued them, till he has conquered the sense of taste. When the sense of taste is subdued, all the senses become controlled.

devanagari: १ पिङ्गला नाम वेश्याऽसीद् विदेहनगरे पुरा । तस्या मे शिक्षितं किञ्चित् निबोध नृपनन्दन ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: 1 piṅgalā nāma veśyā tasyā me śikṣitaṃ kiñcit nibodha nṛpanandana

translation: Formerly, in the city of Videha (i.e Mithilā), there lived a courtesan called Piṅgalā. Please listen to what I have learnt from her O Prince.

devanagari: सा स्वैरिण्येकदा कान्तं सङ्केत उपनेष्यती । अभूत्काले बहिर्द्वारि बिभ्रती रूपमुत्तमम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā svairiṇyekadā kāntaṃ saṅketa upaneṣyatī abhūtkāle bahirdvāri bibhratī rūpamuttamam

translation: On one occasion, the public woman adorned herself to exhibit her charms to the utmost, at the proper time, and waited outside the door with the intention of enticing a paramour to a renḍczous.

devanagari: मार्ग आगच्छतो वीक्ष्य पुरुषान् पुरुषर्षभ । तान् शुल्कदान् वित्तवतः कान्तान् मेनेऽर्थकामुका ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: mārga āgacchato vīkṣya puruṣān puruṣarṣabha tān śulkadān vittavataḥ kāntān mene

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: O prominent Person! As she saw men passing by the way, she, being covetous for money, thought them to be wealthy patrons coming to pay her as her paramours.

devanagari: आगतेष्वपयातेषु सा सङ्केतोपजीविनी । अप्यन्यो वित्तवान् कोऽपि मामुपैष्यति भूरिदः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: āgateṣvapayāteṣu sā saṅketopajīvinī apyanyo vittavān ko
translation: When the passers-by came and went their way (ignoring her), the woman, who earned her living by prostitution, indulged in the (false) hope that some other wealthy man would turn up and could approach her with a rich fee.

devanagari: एवं दुराशया ध्वस्तनिद्रा द्वार्यवलम्बती । निर्गच्छन्ती प्रविशती निशीथं समपद्यत ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ durāśayā dhvastanidrā dvāryavalambatī nirgacchantī praviśatī niśīthaṃ samapadyata
translation: Tn this way, her inordinate hopes being thwarted, she lost her sleep. She kept on standing at the door, going in and out till it was past midnight.

devanagari: तस्या वित्ताशया शुष्यद्वक्त्राया दीनचेतसः । निर्वेदः परमो जज्ञे चिन्ताहेतुः सुखावहः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyā vittāśayā śuṣyadvaktrāyā dīnacetasaḥ nirvedaḥ paramo jajñe cintāhetuḥ sukhāvahaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb]

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Due to frustration of her hopes of earning money, her mouth parched up, and she became dejected at heart. But the despondency caused by her anxiety engendered a sense of real happiness in her.

devanagari: तस्या निर्विण्णचित्ताया गीतं श्रृणु यथा मम । निर्वेद आशापाशानां पुरुषस्य यथा ह्यसिः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyā nirviṇṇacittāyā gītaṃ śrṛṇu yathā mama nirveda āśāpāśānāṃ puruṣasya yathā hyasiḥ
translation: Now listen from me the song of the woman whose mind was utterly frustrated and disgusted: “Complete indifference to worldly objects is like unto a sword in a man’s hand to cut asunder the cords of desire.”

devanagari: न ह्यङ्गाज्जातनिर्वेदो देहबन्धं जिहासति । यथा विज्ञानरहितो मनुजो ममतां नृप ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyaṅgājjātanirvedo dehabandhaṃ jihāsati yathā vijñānarahito manujo mamatāṃ nṛpa

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rah class 10 verb]

translation: Dear King, just as a man without any spiritual knowledge cannot give up his sense of“mineness”, a person in whose mind the sense of complete dispassion (for worldly objects) has not dawned, will not be able to rid himself of the tics to the body.

devanagari: पिङ्गलोवाच । अहो मे मोहविततिं पश्यताविजितात्मनः । या कान्ताद् असतः कामं कामये येन बालिशा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: piṅgalovāca aho me mohavitatiṃ paśyatāvijitātmanaḥ yā kāntād asataḥ kāmaṃ kāmaye yena bāliśā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Piṅgalā said:
Alas! Look at the extent of delusion of mine who have not controlled my mind and senses. Thoughtless as I am, I covet the fulfilment of my desire even from a worthless paramour.

devanagari: सन्तं समीपे रमणं रतिप्रदं । वित्तप्रदं नित्यमिमं विहाय । अकामदं दुःखभयाधिशोक । मोहप्रदं तुच्छमहं भजेऽज्ञा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: santaṃ samīpe ramaṇaṃ ratipradaṃ vittapradaṃ nityamimaṃ vihāya akāmadaṃ duḥkhabhayādhiśoka mohapradaṃ tucchamahaṃ bhaje

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]

translation: Ignoring this eternally proximate paramour (viz. the Indwelling Soul), capable of giving real delight and bestower of sumptuous wealth on me, I, in my infatuation, resorted to a contemptible fellow who is incapable of fulfilling my desires and who confers nothing but misery, fear, grief and delusion.

devanagari: अहो मयाऽत्मा परितापितो वृथा । साङ्केत्यवृत्त्यातिविगर्ह्यवार्तया । स्त्रैणान्नराद् यार्थतृषोऽनुशोच्यात् । क्रीतेन वित्तं रतिमात्मनेच्छत...
english_devnagari: aho mayā sāṅketyavṛttyātivigarhyavārtayā straiṇānnarād yārthatṛṣo krītena vittaṃ ratimātmanecchatī

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 7 verb]
[nominative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Alas I I have uselessly put my soul to affliction by leading the life of a public woman—the most reproachable trade. By selling myself to a miserly pitiable woman-addict I expected to get wealth and pleasure.

devanagari: यदस्थिभिर्निर्मितवंशवंश्य । स्थूणं त्वचा रोमनखैः पिनद्धम् । क्षरन्नवद्वारमगारमेतद् । विण्मूत्रपूर्णं मदुपैति कान्या ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadasthibhirnirmitavaṃśavaṃśya sthūṇaṃ tvacā romanakhaiḥ pinaddham kṣarannavadvāramagārametad viṇmūtrapūrṇaṃ madupaiti kānyā

translation: What person other than myself would seek (for enjoyment) the male body which is like a house, the framework (like beams and rafters) of which is made of bones, covered with skin, hair and nails, provided with nine doors dissipating filth and full of dirty excretion and urine.

devanagari: विदेहानां पुरे ह्यस्मिन् अहमेकैव मूढधीः । यान्यमिच्छन्त्यसत्यस्माद् आत्मदात् काममच्युतात् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: videhānāṃ pure hyasmin ahamekaiva mūḍhadhīḥ yānyamicchantyasatyasmād ātmadāt kāmamacyutāt

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [nominative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ iṣ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: In the whole city of Videha (Mithilā), I am perhaps the only foolish and wicked woman who expect to get pleasure (from persons) other than this Acyuta who confers his own self to devotees.

devanagari: सुहृत्प्रेष्ठतमो नाथ आत्मा चायं शरीरिणाम् । तं विक्रीयात्मनैवाहं रमेऽनेन यथा रमा ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: suhṛtpreṣṭhatamo nātha ātmā cāyaṃ śarīriṇām taṃ vikrīyātmanaivāhaṃ rame
translation: He is the beloved-most friend, protector and the very Self of all embodied beings. I shall submit myself to him and enjoy myself with him like goddess Lakṣmī.

devanagari: कियत् प्रियं ते व्यभजन् कामा ये कामदा नराः । आद्यन्तवन्तो भार्याया देवा वा कालविद्रुताः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kiyat priyaṃ te vyabhajan kāmā ye kāmadā narāḥ ādyantavanto bhāryāyā devā vā kālavidrutāḥ

translation: To what extent can men, subject to birth and death, and gods, who are overtaken by Time, give pleasures to their wives (for all pleasures have a beginning and an end).

devanagari: नूनं मे भगवान् प्रीतो विष्णुः केनापि कर्मणा । निर्वेदोऽयं दुराशाया यन्मे जातः सुखावहः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ me bhagavān prīto viṣṇuḥ kenāpi karmaṇā nirvedo

[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: It appears that Lord Viṣṇu has been pleased with me for some unknown act of mine (in my past life). Hence this dispassion which ultimately leads to happiness has been engendered in my heart, full of evil desires.

devanagari: मैवं स्युः मन्दभाग्यायाः क्लेशा निर्वेदहेतवः । येनानुबन्धं निर्हृत्य पुरुषः शममृच्छति ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: maivaṃ syuḥ mandabhāgyāyāḥ kleśā nirvedahetavaḥ yenānubandhaṃ nirhṛtya puruṣaḥ śamamṛcchati
translation: But for the grace of the Lord the afflictions of an unlucky woman like me would not have caused this feeling of disgust and renunciation which enables a person to cut asunder all attachment, and attains (mental) peace (and spiritual bliss).

devanagari: तेनोपकृतमादाय शिरसा ग्राम्यसङ्गताः । त्यक्त्वा दुराशाः शरणं व्रजामि तमधीश्वरम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tenopakṛtamādāya śirasā grāmyasaṅgatāḥ tyaktvā durāśāḥ śaraṇaṃ vrajāmi tamadhīśvaram
translation: Respectfully receiving on my head, the blessings (and the grace) of the Lord, I shall rid myself of vulgar desires caused by low associations, and seek shelter in the Supreme Lord.

devanagari: सन्तुष्टा श्रद्दधत्येतद् यथालाभेन जीवती । विहरामिमुनैवाहमात्मना रमणेन वै ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: santuṣṭā śraddadhatyetad yathālābhena jīvatī viharāmimunaivāhamātmanā ramaṇena vai

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ jīv class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ jīv class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ jīv class 1 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Contented and subsisting on whatever comes to me by the will of Providence, I shall, with full faith in the Lord who is my own Self, enjoy myself with him as my beloved.

devanagari: संसारकूपे पतितं विषयैर्मुषितेक्षणम् । ग्रस्तं कालाहिनात्मानं कोऽन्यस्त्रातुमधीश्वरः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃsārakūpe patitaṃ viṣayairmuṣitekṣaṇam grastaṃ kālāhinātmānaṃ ko

[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Who else is capable of protecting a person who has fallen in the deep well of Saṃsāra, and whose eyes (power of discrimination) are blinded by objects of senses and whose person is swallowed by the serpent (boa-constrictor) in the form of Time.

devanagari: आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो गोप्ता निर्विद्येत यदाखिलात् । अप्रमत्त इदं पश्येद् ग्रस्तं कालाहिना जगत् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmaiva hyātmano goptā nirvidyeta yadākhilāt apramatta idaṃ paśyed grastaṃ kālāhinā jagat

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ gras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ gras class 1 verb]

translation: When a person observes that this world is practically devoured by the serpent in the form of Time, he becomes alert and is disgusted with all worldly objects. He realizes that his Ātman is the protector of his Self.

devanagari: श्रीब्राह्मण उवाच । एवं व्यवसितमतिः दुराशां कान्ततर्षजाम् । छित्त्वोपशममास्थाय शय्यामुपविवेश सा ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrāhmaṇa uvāca evaṃ vyavasitamatiḥ durāśāṃ kāntatarṣajām chittvopaśamamāsthāya śayyāmupaviveśa sā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Brāhmaṇa (Avadhūta) said:
Coming to this firm conclusion and having thus cut off the evil libidinous craving for paramours, she was established in serenity and took her seat on the bed.

devanagari: आशा हि परमं दुःखं नैराश्यं परमं सुखम् । यथा सञ्छिद्य कान्ताशां सुखं सुष्वाप पिङ्गला ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: āśā hi paramaṃ duḥkhaṃ nairāśyaṃ paramaṃ sukham yathā sañchidya kāntāśāṃ sukhaṃ suṣvāpa piṅgalā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Certainly (the state of being haunted by) hope is the greatest misery and freedom from hope is the happiest state, just as when Piṅgalā gave up all hope for a paramour, she enjoyed a happy sleep.
